bins.name: Non-Firm Interconnection for Flexible Loads
bins.desc: The filing's central argument is that bitcoin mining and AI compute loads can curtail in seconds and should qualify for a dedicated non-firm interconnection pathway. The filer argues this differs fundamentally from always-on load, reduces upgrade requirements, and benefits grid reliability. SPP's HILLS program is cited as a proven template.
quotes: not all large loads behave the same way. Digital-compute data centers, particularly bitcoin mining and, in the future, some emerging AI compute installations, can operate as large, flexible, interruptible loads with the capability to modulate power consumption within seconds in response to grid demands.
we recommend that DOE support the creation and adoption of a non-firm load interconnection category across U.S. markets.
Southwest Power Pool's (SPP) High-Impact Large Loads (HILLs) framework enables "fast-track" interconnection for large loads that agree to curtail when needed to preserve reliability.

Flexible load does not require the same level of transmission upgrades as other businesses and often locates at or near existing power sources. This reduces required studies, transmission upgrades, and queue congestion.

Bitcoin mining facilities in Texas have already demonstrated real-time load shed performance ahead of grid emergencies, contributing meaningful stability during both extreme heat and winter reliability events.

When these loads are exposed to real-time prices and congestion signals, they shift consumption away from peak periods using a variety of market-based tools, reducing uplift charges and lessening the need for emergency procurement.
Encourage FERC-jurisdictional ISOs and RTOs as well as ERCOT to establish a uniform, non-firm interconnection category for qualifying flexible loads.

bins.name: Reduced Study and Queue Burden
bins.desc: Flexible loads site near existing sources and require fewer transmission upgrades, cutting study requirements and queue congestion. The filer also asks DOE to coordinate with NERC to set transparent, achievable performance criteria.
quotes: Flexible load does not require the same level of transmission upgrades as other businesses and often locates at or near existing power sources. This reduces required studies, transmission upgrades, and queue congestion.
Expand coordination with NERC and reliability entities to ensure transparent, achievable performance criteria for fast-acting interruptible load.

bins.name: Consumer Cost and Infrastructure Revenue
bins.desc: The filer argues that flexible loads raise revenue on existing wires without new construction and, when exposed to real-time prices, shift away from peaks and cut uplift charges, lowering costs for all consumers.
quotes: In instances where utilities do not have to build more transmission to connect flexible loads, they can increase revenue on the same infrastructure, wires, and substations. Prior to TDSP rate adjustments, this increased revenue can, in turn, help pay for additional transmission projects assuming guidelines have been developed outlining what transmission charges apply to these loads when they are co-located, at the same POI.

When these loads are exposed to real-time prices and congestion signals, they shift consumption away from peak periods using a variety of market-based tools, reducing uplift charges and lessening the need for emergency procurement.

bins.name: Co-Location Anchors New Generation
bins.desc: Flexible loads can anchor new generation projects and unlock financing in transmission-constrained areas. The filer notes that co-location raises questions about which transmission charges apply at a shared POI.
quotes: In instances where utilities do not have to build more transmission to connect flexible loads, they can increase revenue on the same infrastructure, wires, and substations. Prior to TDSP rate adjustments, this increased revenue can, in turn, help pay for additional transmission projects assuming guidelines have been developed outlining what transmission charges apply to these loads when they are co-located, at the same POI.
Flexible loads can serve as anchor tenants for new generation projects and unlock financing for transmission-constrained generation pockets.

bins.name: SPP HILLS as Template
bins.desc: The filer points to SPP's High-Impact Large Loads framework as an existing fast-track model that other markets should replicate, without criticizing SPP or calling for changes to SPP's own rules.
quotes: Southwest Power Pool's (SPP) High-Impact Large Loads (HILLs) framework enables "fast-track" interconnection for large loads that agree to curtail when needed to preserve reliability.

bins.name: Bitcoin Mining Demand Response Record
bins.desc: The filer highlights that Texas bitcoin miners have already shed load in real time ahead of grid emergencies in both heat and winter events. This track record is offered as evidence that bitcoin mining qualifies as a reliable demand-response resource.
quotes: not all large loads behave the same way. Digital-compute data centers, particularly bitcoin mining and, in the future, some emerging AI compute installations, can operate as large, flexible, interruptible loads with the capability to modulate power consumption within seconds in response to grid demands.

Bitcoin mining facilities in Texas have already demonstrated real-time load shed performance ahead of grid emergencies, contributing meaningful stability during both extreme heat and winter reliability events.
Expand coordination with NERC and reliability entities to ensure transparent, achievable performance criteria for fast-acting interruptible load.

bins.name: Uniform Non-Firm Category Across Markets
bins.desc: The filer asks DOE to encourage both FERC-jurisdictional ISOs and RTOs and non-jurisdictional ERCOT to adopt a common non-firm interconnection category, not just a patchwork of regional pilots.
quotes: we recommend that DOE support the creation and adoption of a non-firm load interconnection category across U.S. markets.
Encourage FERC-jurisdictional ISOs and RTOs as well as ERCOT to establish a uniform, non-firm interconnection category for qualifying flexible loads.
